_meta.name: English
_meta.note: English mirror of i18n/zh.json. Same keys, English values. Param substitution: {name}.

app.title: RelayForge Admin
app.subtitle: v{version} · Local-first AI coding gateway
app.tokenRequiredHint: /admin/* requires Bearer Token. Token is kept in this browser's sessionStorage only. Also accepts RELAY_TOKEN / OPENRELAY_TOKEN for backward compatibility.
app.tokenOptionalHint: Recommended: set RELAYFORGE_TOKEN (or RELAY_TOKEN / OPENRELAY_TOKEN) in .env to protect the admin API.
app.noAuthHint: RelayForge is running without authentication. Do not run in this mode on a machine where untrusted browser tabs can reach 127.0.0.1.
app.tokenCopied: Copied to clipboard (session-only; cleared when the browser tab closes)
common.muted: Local relay does not throttle calls; upstream limits still apply
quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.mode.local.desc: Only local providers configured; no cloud API key needed.
quickstart.mode.cloud.desc: Cloud API key configured, no local provider.
quickstart.mode.mixed.desc: Mix of local models and cloud API keys, automatic fallback on failure.
quickstart.missingKey: Cloud provider missing key: {names}. Add a Web Key on the Provider page or edit .env.
quickstart.untestedLocal: Local provider untested: {names}. Make sure Ollama / LM Studio / vLLM / llama.cpp is running.
quickstart.allOk: Keys and local provider status look good.
quickstart.toolEnvNote: Scripts only set process-level env vars in the current shell, never modify system env vars, Windows registry, or ~/.bashrc.
overview.title: Overview
overview.baseUrlNote: Default bind: 127.0.0.1:{port}, not exposed to LAN or WAN. OpenAI-compatible Base URL = {baseUrl}.
overview.profile.title: Active profile
overview.profile.hint: When request model is auto / default or omitted, this profile's defaultModel is used.
overview.actions.title: Quick actions
overview.actions.toolEnv: Go to Tools to generate tool-env
overview.actions.note: Only sets process-level env vars in the current shell, never modifies system env vars, Windows registry, macOS / Linux /etc/environment, or any ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc.
overview.recentErrors.title: Last 5 errors
providers.title: Providers
providers.list.title: List
providers.filter.empty: No providers match the current filter.
providers.form.title: Create / edit provider
providers.form.note: Real API keys are not saved here, still in the "Add real API key" form below or in .env. Base URL allows https:// or local loopback http:// by default; remote http:// requires explicit allowInsecureHttp.
providers.form.webKeyNote: About Web Keys: Real API keys are encrypted independently in data/keys.enc.json, fully separate from config.json. Clicking "Save" / "Create" does not clear existing Web Keys or overwrite them. Existing Web Keys continue to participate in the key pool rotation. Use the "Added Web Keys" table below ("Disable / Enable / Delete" buttons) to manage them.
providers.form.templatePlaceholder: Pick a template to fill the form
providers.form.name: Provider name
providers.form.allowInsecureHelp: Only enable for trusted on-prem vLLM and similar; remote http:// leaks API keys in plaintext.
providers.form.keyEnv: Key env var name (not the API key)
providers.form.keyEnvPlaceholder: e.g. DEEPSEEK_API_KEYS; use the Web Key form below to add real keys
providers.form.keyEnvHelp: Only the env var name here, e.g. DEEPSEEK_API_KEYS. Local loopback providers don't need keyEnv.
providers.form.hint: Start from a template, or click "Edit" on a provider row above.
providers.discover.title: Discover available models (with Base URL + Key, or use an existing provider's key)
providers.discover.note: Both modes call /models to fetch the upstream model list, then render it as a checkbox list:
providers.discover.modeA: Mode A: any OpenAI-compatible Base URL + ephemeral key — does not save the key, never writes config.json / logs / persistent files, never echoes it back. Base URL must be https:// or local loopback http://.
providers.discover.modeB: Mode B: existing provider — uses the provider's saved key (Web Key first, then .env keyEnv). Does not affect other Web Keys.
providers.discover.modeBLabel: Mode B: pull from configured provider (using its saved key)
providers.discover.placeholder: Not yet discovered. Pick mode A (Base URL + Key) or mode B (provider); returned model IDs render here as checkboxes. Use Select all / none / invert, then "Replace with selection" or "Merge into" the Models field above. Does not auto-save; click "Save changes" to persist.
providers.discover.empty: 0 models returned. The Base URL may be Anthropic-native (chat only) or another non-OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
providers.discover.found: Discovered {count} models from {source} (took {elapsed} ms). Tick the ones to keep:
providers.discover.replace: Replace with selection (overwrite the Models field above)
providers.discover.replaceApplied: Replaced Models field with {count} selected models (remember to click "Save changes").
providers.discover.mergeApplied: Merged {count} selected models into the Models field (kept {left} existing).
providers.discover.prefillFail: Base URL above is empty, cannot prefill.
providers.discover.prefilled: Copied Base URL above into the discovery input.
providers.discover.source.provider: {name} saved key (using keyPool Key to call /models)
providers.discover.apiKeyPlaceholder: sk-... (leave empty for local Ollama / LM Studio)
providers.inlineKey.title: Add a real API key for the current provider
providers.inlineKey.note: Key is encrypted and saved locally to data/keys.enc.json, never written to config.json, exported config, or the release zip.
providers.inlineKey.label: Label (optional)
providers.inlineKey.empty: Key is only encrypted and stored locally; it will not be shown in plaintext on the page.
providers.webKeys.title: Added Web Keys
providers.webKeys.empty: No Web Keys added yet. Use .env or click "Add key" on a provider row.
providers.webKeys.fromForm.title: Add a new key from the API key form
providers.webKeys.col.label: Label
providers.webKeys.deleteConfirm: Delete key {label} (ID {id})? The key is removed from the key pool immediately.
routes.title: Routes / Profiles / Key pool
routes.preview.title: Route preview
routes.preview.readonly: Read-only · no upstream call · no config write
routes.preview.note: Enter a model name (auto / default / route name / provider:model / bare model name) to see which route / profile it matches, candidate order, provider local vs cloud, key availability, health, and the allowInsecureHttp risk flag.
routes.preview.fail: Resolution failed: {status} · This read-only panel never calls upstream and never writes config.
routes.preview.summary: Summary: {total} candidates · {local} local · {cloud} cloud · {needsKey} missing key · {risk} insecure · {failed} unhealthy
routes.list.title: Virtual model groups (Routes)
routes.form.title: Create / edit Route
routes.form.note: Save auto-backs up config.json; the server checks Profile references before deletion. Local relay does not throttle calls (upstream limits still apply); daily limit is a soft cap.
routes.form.templatePlaceholder: Pick a template to fill the form
routes.form.name: Route name
routes.form.descriptionPlaceholder: e.g. Prefer cloud, fall back to local model on failure
routes.form.limitPlaceholder: Leave empty to mean: local relay does not throttle (upstream limits still apply)
routes.form.candidatesNote: Each candidate has provider, model, weight; weight defaults to 1. Real keys are not added here; candidates must reference existing providers.
routes.form.hint: Start from a template, or click "Edit" on a route row above.
routes.form.deleteConfirm: Delete route {name}? The server will refuse if any Profile references it.
routes.profiles.title: Profile presets
routes.profiles.col.name: Profile
routes.keypool.title: Runtime key pool
routes.keypool.note: Masked label and first 12 chars of hash only; keys in cooldown are skipped on next selection.
tools.title: Tools
tools.tokenNote.required: RELAY_TOKEN is set: API key value should be the RELAY_TOKEN value.
tools.tokenNote.optional: RELAY_TOKEN is not set: API key value can be "local".
tools.relayNote: · Local relay does not throttle calls; upstream limits still apply.
tools.generator.title: Tool config generator
tools.generator.readonly: Read-only · no upstream call · no config write
tools.generator.note: Pick a tool + pick a model group (or Route); the panel emits PowerShell / CMD / Bash commands for the current session plus a unified verify command.
tools.generator.verifyNote: Generator only emits process-level shell commands, never writes system env vars, Windows registry, or ~/.bashrc / ~/.zshrc, never puts tokens in URLs, never writes logs.
tools.generator.tailNote: Generated commands only modify the current shell's env vars ($env: / set / export), not system env vars, registry, or shell profile; copy buttons only write to the clipboard and never log.
tools.cards.title: Recommended default config for 8 tools
tools.cards.note: Direct view of each tool's Base URL / key alias / verify method
tools.verify.title: Unified verify script
tools.verify.note: Run powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\write-tool-env.ps1 to generate tool-env.* and tool-verify.*, then .\tool-env.ps1, then .\tool-verify.ps1. The script GETs /v1/models + POSTs /v1/chat/completions (model=auto); both 200 = all 7 tool env-var aliases work.
usage.title: Usage & Errors
usage.today.title: Today's usage
usage.history.title: History
usage.errors.title: Recent errors (newest first)
usage.errors.filter.label: Filter:
usage.errors.filter.empty: No errors match the current filter.
usage.errors.note: Prompts are never shown; only error category, scope, and summary. Categories are server-authoritative; legacy entries without `category` fall back to the heuristic.
usage.errors.empty.title: No errors yet
usage.errors.empty.line1: The relay has not recorded any errors since startup.
usage.errors.empty.line2: Errors appear here when upstream returns 4xx / 5xx, streams idle out, or local soft limits are hit.
settings.title: Settings
settings.token.title: Local admin token
settings.token.note: If RELAY_TOKEN is set, paste the token below to save it to this browser's sessionStorage (not written to file / URL / log). Local usage still works without RELAY_TOKEN, but recommended to protect the admin API in .env.
settings.token.label: RELAY_TOKEN
settings.token.placeholder: Leave empty if RELAY_TOKEN is not set
settings.editor.title: Config editor
settings.editor.note: Edit only non-secret config: providers, model groups, limits, health checks. The editor rejects apiKey / token / secret / password / cookie / authorization fields. Real keys stay in .env or Web Key admin. Save validates JSON and auto-backs up the old config.json.
settings.health.title: Health check cache
settings.health.note: Health checks send a tiny request to upstream and may consume upstream quota; only enable when monitoring is genuinely needed.
settings.balance.title: Balance check cache
settings.balance.note: The "Balance" button on each provider row calls the public balanceEndpoint configured in config.json. Does not poll automatically, does not read any local app's token/cookie. 302 redirects are actively refused.
settings.balance.empty: No balance checks yet. Configure balanceEndpoint on a provider in config.json to enable.
settings.discovery.title: Model discovery cache
settings.discovery.note: The "Discover" button on each provider row calls /models and caches the model list locally. Never modifies config.json automatically.
modal.profile.title: Edit profile
modal.profile.subtitle: Saving auto-backs up config.json.
modal.profile.name: Name
msg.tokenSaved: Local admin token saved to this browser session.
msg.profileMissingName: Internal error: missing original profile name.
msg.profileFieldsRequired: Profile name and default model are required.
msg.providerNext.webKey: Next: add a real key for this provider in the API key form.
msg.providerNext.local: Local provider does not need keyEnv.
msg.providerFieldsRequired: Provider name and Base URL are required.
msg.providerNameRequired: Fill in or pick a provider name first.
msg.routeFieldsRequired: Route name and candidate list are required.
msg.routeNameRequired: Fill in or pick a route name first.
msg.keySaved: Saved: ID {id}, masked {mask} · This provider now has {count} Web Keys. Web Keys are encrypted independently; editing / saving the provider config above does not affect them.
msg.keyChosen: Picked provider: {name}. Paste the real API key here.
msg.keyChosenInline: Picked provider: {name}. The key will be encrypted and not written to config.json.
msg.keyTestAfter: Key saved, running a minimal connectivity test; cloud providers may consume a small amount of quota...
msg.profileDeleteConfirm: Delete profile {name}? The active profile cannot be deleted.
msg.providerDeleteConfirm: Delete provider {name}? The server will refuse if any model group, Profile, or Web Key still uses it.
msg.errorProviderKeyEnv: keyEnv can only hold an env var name. Looks like a real API key; use the API key admin to add it.
